_note: Per-lesson role-play scenario. Grounded in A2/012 dialogue + vocab. Learner plays Ben; computer plays Sam. Each NPC node offers 2-3 branches so the same lesson language can be reached different ways. Consumed by RP.Engine + RP.classifiers.local (see roleplay/).
title: 012 · The Football Match

npc.text: Are you ready for the football match?

learning_objective: Say you are ready and confident

routes.meaning: Ben is ready and thinks they will win.
examples: Yes! I think we will win today.
Yes! I think we will win.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Yes! I think we will win today.”
You can say “Yes! I think we will win today.”

partial.message: Show you're ready: “Yes! I think we will win today.”

off_topic.message: It's match time! Say “Yes! I think we will win today.”

npc.text: Don't worry! We are a good team. Do you think we can win?

routes.meaning: Ben says yes, they will win.
examples: Yes! I think we will win today.
Yes, I think we will win.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Yes! I think we will win today.”

partial.message: Say “Yes! I think we will win today.”

off_topic.message: Tell Sam “I think we will win today.”

npc.text: Why do you think we will win?

routes.meaning: Ben says because they practiced hard.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Because we practiced very hard.”

routes.meaning: Ben says because the team is strong.
examples: Because we are a strong team.
routes.hint: You can say “Because we practiced very hard.”

partial.message: Give your reason: “Because we practiced very hard.”

off_topic.message: Sam asked why. Say “Because we practiced very hard.”

npc.text: True, we are strong! And we trained a lot, right?

examples: Yes, because we practiced very hard.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “Because we practiced very hard.”

npc.text: Who is the best player on our team?

routes.meaning: Ben says Sam is the best and runs the fastest.
examples: You are! You run the fastest.
You are! You run the fastest.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “You are! You run the fastest.”

routes.meaning: Ben says Sam is the best player.
routes.hint: You can add why: “You are! You run the fastest.”

partial.message: Praise Sam: “You are! You run the fastest.”

off_topic.message: Sam asked who's best. Say “You are! You run the fastest.”

npc.text: Me? Thanks! But you are fast too. What can I do best?

routes.meaning: Ben says Sam runs the fastest.
examples: You are! You run the fastest.

npc.text: Thanks! You score the most goals.

routes.meaning: Ben says if they play together they will be strong.
examples: If we play together, we will be strong.
If we work together, we will be strong.
If we play together, we will win.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “If we play together, we will be strong.”

routes.meaning: Ben thanks Sam for the compliment.
examples: Thank you! That makes me happy.
routes.hint: You can say “If we play together, we will be strong.”

partial.message: Talk teamwork: “If we play together, we will be strong.”

off_topic.message: You score the goals! Say “If we play together, we will be strong.”

npc.text: You're welcome! So how can we win the game?

routes.meaning: Ben says they must play together.
examples: If we play together, we will be strong.
If we play together, we will win.
routes.hint: 💬 Try saying: “If we play together, we will be strong.”

partial.message: Say “If we play together, we will be strong.”

off_topic.message: Tell Sam “If we play together, we will be strong.”

routes.meaning: Ben cheers that they can do it.
routes.hint: You can cheer, or say “Go team, go!”

off_topic.message: Time to cheer! Say “Go team, go!”

npc.text: That's the spirit! Now let's cheer!
Go team, go! Let's win this match!
